title: Mahananda Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Mahananda Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of West Bengal

overview.short: Mahananda Wildlife Sanctuary (Pron: móhɑ́nɑ́ndaa) is located on the foothills of the Himalayas, between the Teesta and Mahananda rivers.
overview.description: Mahananda Wildlife Sanctuary (Pron: móhɑ́nɑ́ndaa) is located on the foothills of the Himalayas, between the Teesta and Mahananda rivers. Situated in the Darjeeling district of West Bengal, India; it comes under Darjeeling Wildlife division and can be reached from Siliguri in 30 minutes. Sukna, the gateway to the sanctuary, is only 13 km from Siliguri and 28 km from Bagdogra airport. The sanctuary sprawls over 159 km2 of reserve forest and was started as a game sanctuary in 1955.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Sivok Junction
topPlaces.description: Sivok Junction also known as Sevoke Junction is a junction railway station in Darjeeling district, West Bengal. Its code is SVQ. The station serves Sevoke town.

topPlaces.name: Sevoke
topPlaces.description: Sevoke (also Sevok or Sivok) is a town near Siliguri in the Darjeeling district of West Bengal state of India near the border with Sikkim state. Situated in Dooars, Sevoke lies on the bank of River Teesta and has two bridges − namely Coronation Bridge and Sevoke Railway Bridge over it.

topPlaces.name: Sevoke Railway Bridge
topPlaces.description: Sevoke Railway Bridge is the railway bridge on River Teesta near Mahananda Wildlife Sanctuary at Sevoke, West Bengal, India. The railway bridge is about 396.1 metres long and connects Darjeeling district with Jalpaiguri district of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Coronation Bridge
topPlaces.description: The Coronation Bridge, also known as the Sevoke Roadway Bridge, is a bridge across the Teesta River at Sevoke in the Indian state of West Bengal. The bridge is a part of the National Highway 17 (previously National Highway 31), and connects the districts of Darjeeling and Kalimpong.

topPlaces.name: Kalijhora
topPlaces.description: Kalijhora is an urban village in the Kalimpong I CD block that lies on the bank of River Teesta, in the Kalimpong Sadar subdivision of the Kalimpong district in the state of West Bengal, India. National Highway 10 connecting Siliguri and Gangtok passes through Kalijhora.

topPlaces.name: Mongpong
topPlaces.description: Mongpong is a village in Kalimpong I, a community development block in India in the Kalimpong subdivision of the Kalimpong district of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Teesta Low Dam - IV Hydropower Plant
topPlaces.description: Teesta Low Dam - IV Hydropower Plant is a run-of-the-river hydroelectric station built on the Teesta River at Kalijhora, Kalimpong district, West Bengal.

image.alt: Teesta Low Dam - IV Hydropower Plant

items.note: Sivok Junction also known as Sevoke Junction is a junction railway station in Darjeeling district, West Bengal. Its code is SVQ. The station serves Sevoke town.

Sevoke (also Sevok or Sivok) is a town near Siliguri in the Darjeeling district of West Bengal state of India near the border with Sikkim state. Situated in Dooars, Sevoke lies on the bank of River Teesta and has two bridges − namely Coronation Bridge and Sevoke Railway Bridge over it.

Sevoke Railway Bridge is the railway bridge on River Teesta near Mahananda Wildlife Sanctuary at Sevoke, West Bengal, India. The railway bridge is about 396.1 metres long and connects Darjeeling district with Jalpaiguri district of West Bengal.

The Coronation Bridge, also known as the Sevoke Roadway Bridge, is a bridge across the Teesta River at Sevoke in the Indian state of West Bengal. The bridge is a part of the National Highway 17 (previously National Highway 31), and connects the districts of Darjeeling and Kalimpong.

Kalijhora is an urban village in the Kalimpong I CD block that lies on the bank of River Teesta, in the Kalimpong Sadar subdivision of the Kalimpong district in the state of West Bengal, India. National Highway 10 connecting Siliguri and Gangtok passes through Kalijhora.
Mongpong is a village in Kalimpong I, a community development block in India in the Kalimpong subdivision of the Kalimpong district of West Bengal.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Mahananda Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Mahananda Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Mahananda Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Bagdogra Airport (Siliguri/Darjeeling) (24 km) and the nearest railway station is Siliguri Junction (18 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Mahananda Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Mahananda Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Mahananda Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Mahananda Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Mahananda Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sivok Junction, Sevoke, Sevoke Railway Bridge, Coronation Bridge, Kalijhora.
